# Daily Report — 2026-05-26

## Task Completed
Added `get_sla_breach` tool — retrieves a single SLA breach tracking record from the
`task_sla` table by its `sys_id`.  The tool:
- Accepts a `task_sla_id` (32-char hex sys_id) parameter
- Issues a direct `GET /api/now/table/task_sla/{sys_id}` request with display values and
  reference-link exclusion
- Returns normalised fields: `sys_id`, `task`, `sla`, `stage`, `has_breached`,
  `breach_time`, `start_time`, `end_time`, `business_duration`, `duration`,
  `percentage`, `table_name`, `created_on`, `updated_on`
- Handles 404 (not found), empty result, and HTTP errors gracefully
- Registered in `service_desk`, `system_administrator`, and `full` tool packages

## Test Results
- Total tests: 1,241
- Coverage: 91%
- All passing: yes

## Security
Pre-existing environment package advisories only (pyjwt, setuptools, urllib3, wheel).
No vulnerabilities introduced by this change.

## Files Changed
- `src/servicenow_mcp/tools/sla_tools.py` — added `GetSLABreachParams` model and `get_sla_breach` function
- `src/servicenow_mcp/tools/__init__.py` — exported `get_sla_breach`
- `src/servicenow_mcp/utils/tool_utils.py` — imported and registered tool definition
- `config/tool_packages.yaml` — added to service_desk, system_administrator, full packages
- `tests/test_sla_tools.py` — added 10 new tests for `get_sla_breach`

## Next Up
Add `list_sla_breach_definitions` shortcut (list_slas filtered to active breach-capable SLAs)
